subject: Product Management
title: The Pivot
description: A quick, practical guide to The Pivot.

hook.text: Twitter was a podcasting platform. YouTube was a video dating site. Slack was a video game. The pivot made them legendary.

buildup.text: A pivot means changing direction while keeping one foot planted — reusing what you've learned to find a better product.

discovery.text: Odeo was failing as a podcast company when Apple launched its own podcast feature. The team built Twitter as a side project.

twist.text: Not all pivots work. Most fail. But the best ones use existing technology, team knowledge, and user insights in a new direction.

climax.text: The courage to pivot — to admit the current path isn't working — is one of the hardest product decisions a team can make.

punchline.text: Sometimes the best product is hiding inside a failure.

quiz.question: What makes a successful pivot?
options: Reusing existing technology and insights to find a better product direction
